main.name: Will Powell
main.bio: I am a data scientist, currently building machine learning models, and integrating AI automations for a credit risk company. I am consistently engaged in various projects and try to make an effort to keep them update to date here.

education.description: Distinction and top of cohort. Received Dean’s List (2024) for outstanding academic achievement. See the Portfolio section for my thesis.

education.degree: Bachelors in Integrated Mechanical and Electrical Engineering
education.description: First Class Honours and top of cohort. See the Portfolio section for my dissertation.
Higher Levels: Maths, Physics and Biology. Standard Levels: Russian, Psychology, English.

work.title: Data Scientist
work.description: Lendable is a highly innovative credit risk company that is rapidly growing in the UK and US markets for credit cards, unsecured loans, and auto loans. I’m working as a data scientist - building machine learning models to detect risk as well as integrating AI-driven automations for scalable operations.

work.title: Machine Learning Researcher Intern
work.description: MixedBread in developing state-of-the-art embedding models which account for 5% of all model downloads on Hugging Face, and widely adopted by industry leaders such as Google and IBM to help with retrieval and RAG. During my time at MixedBread, I contributed to the research, training, and deployment of these multi-modal models, preparing for a SAAS product release.

work.title: Research Engineer (Part-Time)
work.description: At BrainPatch, a cutting-edge neurotech start-up building an app store for the brain, I designed a wireless EEG (brain) & ECG (heart) recording device from the ground up - hardware, firmware, software, and machine learning pipeline. I developed firmware to provide real-time streaming to the cloud, built a web app, and implemented ML inference on the cloud. The system enabled real-time monitoring during research trials and will power a commercial ML product for stress detection - also the focus of my Bachelor’s and Master’s research.

work.title: Software Engineer
work.description: AB Dynamics is an automotive testing company, with a vast range of products from steering robots to driving simulators. I joined as a software engineer and was the embedded lead for a new product, which was shipped to customers in six months. In parallel, inspired by BrainPatch’s technology, I explored the use of vestibular stimulation to simulate motion in a static driving simulator. I designed and developed the complete software and firmware stack to interface the simulator’s virtual environment with real-time vestibular output, enabling users to perceive rotational forces while driving, and mitigate the common problem of VR nausea. Ultimately, regulation regarding the stimulation put this project on pause, but BrainPatch will continue this investigation having recently got regulatory approval for their stimulators.

work.title: Founder
work.description: After being frustrated with my friends' (and my own) obsession with smartphones, I created a start-up, PhoneCave, that aims to get people off their phones. I wanted to design a gadget that can be used for social situations, to have conversations without distractions, to improve productivity and mindfulness. PhoneCave is a simple idea - a lock box for your phone, keeping it out of reach for as long as you set it. I learnt enormously from starting this business – greatly improving my programming, CAD, electronics and PCB design. I also learnt new skills like 3d printing manufacturing, negotiating with suppliers, web designing and marketing. I shipped PhoneCaves to over 10 countries worldwide, held a promotional campaign on kickstarter and seeked investors. Ultimately, this startup was put on hold as it required significant funding for scaling the manufacturing and at the time I did not back my engineering skills highly enough to take that leap.

projects.title: Truly Modular Multimodal Stress Detection System.
projects.category: My Master's Thesis: Rethinking Stress Monitoring: Convenient Modular Early-Onset Multimodal Stress Detection with Attention Score Caching

projects.title: EEG Recording Device with Machine Learning for Stress Classification.
projects.category: My Bachelor's Dissertation: Design and Implementation of a Single-Lead Chest Strap ECG Recorder for Stress Classification using Machine Learning Methods.

projects.title: MASE: Machine-Learning Accelerator System Exploration Tools.
projects.category: Contributed to an open-source project that aims to combat the pace of development of ML software models which is much faster than accelerator design cycle, rendering many accelerators obsolete.

projects.title: Design of a Self-Organising Multi-Agent System
projects.category: Working alongside 70+ students, I helped to lead the infrastructure team to design an environment for a Self-Organising Multi-Agent system to operate in, written in Go. Six publications are now being written using this system.

projects.title: PhoneCave
projects.category: My first start-up. Check out its website.

projects.title: Neuro-Tech for Driving Simulators
projects.category: Enhancing the Realism of Driving Simulators using Galvanic Vestibular Stimulation Motion Cueing.

projects.title: Chess AI
projects.category: Building a Chess Engine with AI in Python. Using Minimax Recursion, Negamax and Alpha Beta Pruning for computational optimisation.

projects.title: Space Shuttle Tile
projects.category: Calculating the minimum tile thickness of a heat resistant tile during re-entry to earth's atmosphere. Investigation into numerical approximation methods derived from Partial Differential Equations. Modelling and GUI written in MATLAB.

projects.title: Project built for the STM32 using MBED-OS
projects.category: Since most of my projects are kept under the hood in private repositories, I thought I'd share this Uni project which doesn't have any NDAs.

projects.title: Why the Electrification of Vehicles Holds a Large Threat for the Survival of Legacy Automotive Companies
projects.category: My reasoning as to why electrification may be the grand reset for the automotive market.

projects.title: DoorDash Labs - Robotics and Automation of Home Deliveries
projects.category: DoorDash is an impressive company. Started by 4 Staford Grads in 2013 this country now has a market cap of $24Bn. DoorDash is a perfect demonstrator of how to scale a buisness. I am intrigued what their new robots division, DoorDash Labs, will do for the company.
